title: Synapse — browser deep-learning lab
description: A deep-learning framework built from scratch on a real reverse-mode tensor autograd engine (no TensorFlow.js) — 30+ hand-derived ops including conv2d, max/avg pooling, LayerNorm, BatchNorm, dropout, GELU/SiLU, embedding and a full softmax Jacobian, every one proven correct by a one-click engine self-test that gradchecks it (and a whole Transformer end-to-end) to ~1e-7. Three labs share the engine: a 2-D Playground (sketch an MLP, train it live on spirals/checkerboards with AdamW/Nesterov, schedules and clipping, and watch decision boundaries, neuron feature maps, train-vs-val curves and a live computation graph); a Vision lab that trains a from-scratch CNN on a fully procedural image dataset (handwritten-style digits 0–9 and shapes) with live filter/feature-map views, a confusion matrix and a draw-your-own pad; and a Transformer · Attention lab that trains a from-scratch decoder-only GPT on procedural algorithmic tasks (copy/reverse/sort/add) and shows the live per-layer, per-head causal attention maps, greedy decoding coloured against ground truth, a type-your-own decoder with confidence bars, and a token-embedding PCA. Save, load and share experiments via a URL link.
